## [WindowExtWindows](#)

## [In tao::platform::windows](index.html)

# Trait WindowExtWindows Copy item path

`pub trait WindowExtWindows {
// Required methods
fn hinstance(&self) -> isize;
 fn hwnd(&self) -> isize;
 fn set_enable(&self, enabled: bool);
 fn set_taskbar_icon(&self, taskbar_icon: Option<Icon>);
 fn set_overlay_icon(&self, icon: Option<&Icon>);
 fn theme(&self) -> Theme;
 fn reset_dead_keys(&self);
 fn begin_resize_drag(&self, edge: isize, button: u32, x: i32, y: i32);
 fn set_skip_taskbar(&self, skip: bool) -> Result<(), ExternalError>;
 fn set_undecorated_shadow(&self, shadow: bool);
 fn has_undecorated_shadow(&self) -> bool;
 fn set_rtl(&self, rtl: bool);
}`

Additional methods on `Window` that are specific to Windows.

`Window`

## Required Methods[§](#required-methods)

#### fn [hinstance](#tymethod.hinstance)(&self) -> [isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html)

Returns the HINSTANCE of the window

#### fn [hwnd](#tymethod.hwnd)(&self) -> [isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html)

Returns the native handle that is used by this window.

The pointer will become invalid when the native window was destroyed.

#### fn [set\_enable](#tymethod.set_enable)(&self, enabled: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html))

Enables or disables mouse and keyboard input to the specified window.

A window must be enabled before it can be activated.
If an application has create a modal dialog box by disabling its owner window
(as described in [`WindowBuilderExtWindows::with_owner_window`](trait.WindowBuilderExtWindows.html#tymethod.with_owner_window "method tao::platform::windows::WindowBuilderExtWindows::with_owner_window")), the application must enable
the owner window before destroying the dialog box.
Otherwise, another window will receive the keyboard focus and be activated.

`WindowBuilderExtWindows::with_owner_window`

If a child window is disabled, it is ignored when the system tries to determine which
window should receive mouse messages.

For more information, see <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/winuser/nf-winuser-enablewindow#remarks>
and <https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/winmsg/window-features#disabled-windows>

#### fn [set\_taskbar\_icon](#tymethod.set_taskbar_icon)(&self, taskbar\_icon: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")<[Icon](../../window/struct.Icon.html "struct tao::window::Icon")>)

This sets `ICON_BIG`. A good ceiling here is 256x256.

`ICON_BIG`

#### fn [set\_overlay\_icon](#tymethod.set_overlay_icon)(&self, icon: [Option](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/option/enum.Option.html "enum core::option::Option")<&[Icon](../../window/struct.Icon.html "struct tao::window::Icon")>)

This sets the overlay icon

#### fn [theme](#tymethod.theme)(&self) -> [Theme](../../window/enum.Theme.html "enum tao::window::Theme")

Returns the current window theme.

#### fn [reset\_dead\_keys](#tymethod.reset_dead_keys)(&self)

Reset the dead key state of the keyboard.

This is useful when a dead key is bound to trigger an action. Then
this function can be called to reset the dead key state so that
follow-up text input won’t be affected by the dead key.

#### fn [begin\_resize\_drag](#tymethod.begin_resize_drag)(&self, edge: [isize](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.isize.html), button: [u32](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.u32.html), x: [i32](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.i32.html), y: [i32](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.i32.html))

Starts the resizing drag from given edge

#### fn [set\_skip\_taskbar](#tymethod.set_skip_taskbar)(&self, skip: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)) -> [Result](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/result/enum.Result.html "enum core::result::Result")<[()](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.unit.html), [ExternalError](../../error/enum.ExternalError.html "enum tao::error::ExternalError")>

Whether to show the window icon in the taskbar or not.

#### fn [set\_undecorated\_shadow](#tymethod.set_undecorated_shadow)(&self, shadow: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html))

Shows or hides the background drop shadow for undecorated windows.

Enabling the shadow causes a thin 1px line to appear on the top of the window.

#### fn [has\_undecorated\_shadow](#tymethod.has_undecorated_shadow)(&self) -> [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html)

Returns whether this window has shadow for undecorated windows.

#### fn [set\_rtl](#tymethod.set_rtl)(&self, rtl: [bool](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/std/primitive.bool.html))

Sets right-to-left layout.

Enabling this mainly flips the orientation of menus and title bar buttons

## Dyn Compatibility[§](#dyn-compatibility)

This trait **is** [dyn compatible](https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/reference/items/traits.html#dyn-compatibility).

*In older versions of Rust, dyn compatibility was called "object safety".*

## Implementors[§](#implementors)

### impl [WindowExtWindows](trait.WindowExtWindows.html "trait tao::platform::windows::WindowExtWindows") for [Window](../../window/struct.Window.html "struct tao::window::Window")
